overview: This archetype leverages artifact recursion and flash-enabled artifact deployment to assemble recursive loops quickly. By cycling cheap permanents through the graveyard and battlefield, it generates overwhelming value or infinite resources before opponents can stabilize. The W/U/R color scheme provides efficient mana acceleration and versatile interaction to protect the combo.
win_conditions: Assembling a recursive artifact loop for infinite mana and turns
Overwhelming the board with cheap artifact creatures and recursion
Executing a flash-enabled artifact combo on the opponent's turn

keep_criteria: Hands containing Lurrus of the Dream-Den paired with cheap artifacts or creatures
Hands with Goblin Welder or Goblin Engineer alongside Vault Key
Hands with multiple mana rocks and artifact recursion pieces

Keep hands with 2-3 lands (prioritize duals/shocklands or basics that hit WUR), a Lurrus or early threat (Goblin Guide/Monastery Swiftspear), and at least one interaction (Inventor's Inspiration, Lightning Strike, or Spell Pierce).
Keep hands that can cast Lurrus on turn 2 or 3.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking artifact recursion or combo enablers
Hands with only high-cost permanents and no acceleration
Hands without interaction or mana base consistency
Mulligan on 3+ lands with no early plays, or 2+ lands with only removal and no Lurrus/threats.

early_game: Prioritize playing Vault Key to enable flash artifact deployment
Establish mana acceleration with cheap artifact permanents
Set up Goblin Engineer to bypass casting costs for key artifacts
Turn 1: Play a land, cast a 1-drop (Goblin Guide, Monastery Swiftspear, or Spell Pierce/Inventor's Inspiration).
Turn 2: Cast Lurrus if possible, or play a second land and hold for interaction.
Against aggro, prioritize early threats and spot removal.
Against control/combo, protect Lurrus and look for early interaction to enable turn 3-4 combo.
mid_game: Use Goblin Welder to exile unwanted artifacts and enter combo pieces
Recycle Lurrus of the Dream-Den or other key permanents from the graveyard
Apply pressure while assembling the recursive loop
Preserve Lurrus as your primary engine
avoid casting Welder/Engineer unless you can safely assemble the combo or have a clear win path.
Manage the graveyard with Surgical Extraction or discard to prevent opponent recursion.
Use Inventor's Inspiration to cheat on mana for combo pieces or Lurrus.
Commit threats only when you have interaction or a clear win condition.
Hold back Lurrus/Welder/Engineer if the board is unstable or lacks protection.
late_game: Execute the artifact recursion combo for infinite resources
Stabilize with infinite mana to cast game-ending spells
Maintain board presence with recurring artifact creatures

The Goblin Engineer + Goblin Welder + Lightning Greaves infinite draw combo, followed by casting Vault of the Archangel for life gain and card advantage, or using infinite resources to cast a game-winning spell.
Alternatively, win with Monastery Swiftspear or Goblin Guide if the combo is disrupted.
Go all-in when you have protection (Greaves/Boots), a clear path to assemble the combo, and no immediate board wipes or graveyard hate in play.
Commit to the combo if you can resolve the first Engineer and have a way to protect it.

key_cards.name: Lurrus of the Dream-Den
key_cards.notes: Return cheap artifacts or creatures to hand to replay them, fueling recursion loops and maintaining board presence.

key_cards.name: Goblin Welder
key_cards.notes: Exile problematic artifacts or swap them for combo pieces, enabling rapid assembly of recursive loops.

key_cards.name: Goblin Engineer
key_cards.notes: Tap to put artifacts onto the battlefield from hand, bypassing mana costs and enabling surprise plays.

key_cards.name: Vault Key
key_cards.notes: Grants flash to artifacts, allowing you to play combo pieces or recursion tools on the opponent's turn to catch them off guard.

sequencing_tips: Play Vault Key as early as possible to unlock flash artifact deployment
Use Goblin Engineer to get key artifacts onto the battlefield before activating Goblin Welder
Recycle Lurrus of the Dream-Den from the graveyard to replay it or fetch other cheap permanents
Hold interaction in hand to protect combo pieces until the loop is fully assembled

The infinite combo requires precise sequencing: cast Goblin Engineer, attach Lightning Greaves, tap Engineer to sacrifice a 1/1 token (or any creature), draw 2, Engineer dies, Goblin Welder finds another Engineer, repeat.
Use Inventor's Inspiration to cast Engineer for free.
Be aware of graveyard hate and counterspells.
Lurrus is your win condition enabler, so protect it at all costs.
Avoid overcommitting to the combo without protection.

matchups.advice: Play around counterspells and board wipes by holding up interaction and using flash artifacts on their turn. Prioritize recursion to recover from sweepers.
Race their combo by assembling your loop quickly. Use disruption to delay their setup and leverage flash artifacts to interrupt their sequence.
Stabilize with cheap artifact creatures and recursion. Use Goblin Welder to remove their key threats and assemble your combo behind a defensive board.

Use Inventor's Inspiration to cast combo pieces for free, or split the combo across multiple turns. Use Lightning Greaves to protect key creatures from targeted counterspells. Cast Goblin Engineer in response to counterspells when possible.

Prioritize early interaction (Spell Pierce, Lightning Strike) to disrupt mana dorks. Use Surgical Extraction to remove key ramp pieces from the graveyard. If they outpace you, commit to the combo early or win with early threats.

Win the resource war. Prioritize counterspells and removal. Use Lightning Greaves to protect combo pieces. Track Lurrus on the stack. Win by assembling the combo first or out-resolving their Lurrus plays.

common_threats: Watch for graveyard hate (Leyline of the Void, Rest in Peace), counterspells (Spell Pierce, Negate), and board wipes (Supreme Verdict, Anger of the Gods).
Track opponent's hand size and graveyard contents.
Note if they're playing Oath of Druids (early mana dorks) or Dredge (Bitter Ordeal, Golgari Grave-Troll).
